title: salvia lavandulifolia subsp. maurorum
intro: Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. maurorum, also known as the red sage or Maurorum salvia, is a plant species belonging to the sage genus and native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is traditionally used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. maurorum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. maurorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
